summary: Whitney Wolfe Herd discusses the founding of Bumble after experiencing online harassment and a toxic exit from Tinder. She explains how Bumble's 'women make the first move' mechanic aims to disrupt disempowering gender dynamics in dating and business. She also emphasizes that true feminism requires equality for everyone, warning against alienating supportive men.

principles.statement: True feminism means equality for everyone, not just women supporting women.
principles.rationale: Alienating 'the good guys' goes against the core of feminism. Men can be great mentors, and women can be just as problematic or mean as anyone else.
principles.source_quote: It’s not really living up to true feminism, which is really equality for everyone, right?

principles.statement: Entrepreneurship should stem from solving a personal pain point.
principles.rationale: The drive to make a real difference comes from wanting to fix something you've personally experienced and disliked.
principles.source_quote: The hard part is making the difference and the difference starts from within, wanting to change something you’ve personally experienced that you don’t like.

principles.statement: Do not let online abuse or setbacks force you out of your chosen industry.
principles.rationale: Giving up in the face of harassment sets a bad example for younger generations. Tech is the future, and women belong there.
principles.source_quote: Why should I, as a young woman of 24/25, have to give up my career because of a setback, because someone on the internet said something ... If I were to have done that, what kind of example would that have set to my little sister, my friends, my small cousins?

mental_models.name: The First Move Dynamic
mental_models.description: Shifting traditional power dynamics by forcing the historically disempowered party (women) to initiate contact, thereby building confidence and leveling the playing field.
mental_models.example: Bumble requires women to make the first move in dating, and Bumble Bizz applies this same dynamic to professional networking to prevent women from losing confidence in male-dominated spaces.

heuristics: Fix what you personally experience as broken.
Show zero-tolerance for misogyny and small-minded behavior.

signature_quotes.text: I just looked at what it felt like to be a young woman dating and realised how broken it was.
signature_quotes.context: Discussing the inspiration behind Bumble's core mechanics.

signature_quotes.text: Walking into a business meeting, oftentimes [women are] well-educated, smart, talented, capable and you put a bunch of men in the room and for some reason we lose a bit of our confidence, it can be this disempowering experience.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining how the expectation for men to make the first move spans far beyond dating.

signature_quotes.text: The problem doesn’t really start in the office, it starts in the classroom.
signature_quotes.context: Addressing the root cause of the tech industry's gender imbalance.

signature_quotes.text: We as women, [with] this modern feminism, I’m worried we’re alienating the good guys. It’s not really living up to true feminism, which is really equality for everyone, right?
signature_quotes.context: Discussing the importance of male mentors and allies in her career.

signature_quotes.text: The hard part is making the difference and the difference starts from within, wanting to change something you’ve personally experienced that you don’t like.

anti_patterns.description: Alienating men in the name of modern feminism.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It creates a divide and ignores that true feminism is about equality for everyone. It also dismisses the fact that men can be valuable mentors and allies.

anti_patterns.description: Assuming the tech industry's gender imbalance starts solely in the hiring process.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: The root cause is in the education system, where math and science programs are dominated by male students, leading to fewer female applicants for tech roles.

anti_patterns.description: Believing the notion that women only need to support women.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It creates an unrealistic binary. Women can be highly problematic and mean, while men can be incredibly supportive mentors.
